title: Control-M Job Output Scanning for Business-Critical Keywords
dataSources: `index=batch` `sourcetype=controlm:job` with captured `job_output` snippet, or sidecar `controlm:job:output` events
app: Control-M output collection via log adapter, SFTP drop, or Automation API
spl:
index=batch sourcetype="controlm:job"
| search job_output="*POSTING*FAILED*" OR job_output="*BALANCE*MISMATCH*" OR job_output="*ORA-*" OR job_output="*DEADLOCK*"
| rex field=job_output max_match=20 "(?<hit>(?i)posting failed|balance mismatch|ORA-[0-9]{5}|deadlock)"
| stats count, values(job_name) as jobs, latest(order_id) as last_order by hit, folder
| sort -count
description: Many batch jobs exit zero while printing fatal business errors in stdout; keyword monitoring on captured output catches financial and data-integrity failures that exit codes miss. Splunk makes those strings first-class searchable evidence.
value: Closes the gap between technical success and business correctness for ledger, settlement, and ERP extracts.
implementation: Trim output to last N KB to control license cost; store full logs in cheap object storage with a pointer field. Maintain `critical_phrases.csv` lookup and use `lookup` instead of hard-coded OR chain for maintainability.
visualization: Top keyword bar chart, job output viewer panel, trend of hits per folder.
grandmaExplanation: We watch batch and control jobs so missed handoffs or resource limits do not break business processing.
knownFalsePositives: Transient errors during platform maintenance, network blips, or certificate renewals on shared services.
